information:
## Open Bracket Format (OBF) - Complete Reference

Open JSON schema standard for entity vs entity tournament bracket data. v1.0 current. Repo: github.com/openbracketformat/openbracketformat, 36 stars.

ROOT STRUCTURE: { event(required), entrants[](required), sets[](required), version?(v0.1|v0.2|v1.0) }

EVENT: name(required), date(ISO8601), gameName, tournamentStructure(single-elim|double-elim|round-robin), phases[], ruleset, originURL, numberEntrants, other(custom fields)

ENTRANT: entrantID(required), entrantTag(display name), initialSeed(1=best), finalPlacement, personalInformation[{name,country,tag,prefix}], other

SET(MATCH): setID(required), entrant1ID/entrant2ID, status(completed|started|pending), entrant1Result/entrant2Result(win|lose|draw|dq), entrant1Score/entrant2Score(int), entrant1NextSetID/entrant2NextSetID(bracket reconstruction links), setFormat(bestof3 etc), phaseID, roundID, games[], other

GAME: gameNumber(required), entrant1Characters/entrant2Characters(array of strings), stage, entrant1Result/entrant2Result(win|lose|draw|dq), other

KEY GOTCHAS: Sets are FLAT unordered array - bracket reconstructed from nextSetID links. Almost ALL fields optional except root keys, setID, entrantID, event.name. other object pattern for extensibility.

IMPLEMENTATIONS: mtch.gg (full), OBF Web Visualizer, Challonge/Startgg OBF Exporter (github.com/SuperSoma/OBFexporter converts start.gg TO OBF). obf-rs Rust library. NO Python library on PyPI.

## start.gg API - Complete Reference

API Type: GraphQL, single endpoint POST https://api.start.gg/gql/alpha. Request body: { query, operationName?, variables? }

AUTHENTICATION:
- Personal Access Token: Create at start.gg/admin/profile/developer. Header: Authorization: Bearer [token]. Expires 1 year, copy immediately (cannot view again).
- OAuth 2.0: Auth code flow. Auth URL: start.gg/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=&scope=&redirect_uri=. Token exchange: POST api.start.gg/oauth/access_token with grant_type=authorization_code, client_secret, code, scope, client_id, redirect_uri (JSON body). Refresh: POST api.start.gg/oauth/refresh. Response gives access_token (Bearer), expires_in 604800s, refresh_token.
- Scopes: user.identity, user.email, tournament.manager, tournament.reporter

RATE LIMITS: 80 requests/60sec average, 1000 objects max per request. Exceed returns {success:false, message:Rate limit exceeded}.

PAGINATION: page + perPage params. Response has pageInfo{total,totalPages} and nodes[].

DATA MODEL: League > Tournament > Event > Phase > PhaseGroup(Pool) > Set(Match) > Game

KEY QUERIES:
- tournaments(query:{filter:{upcoming:true,videogameIds:[id]},sortBy:startAt asc,perPage:N,page:N}) - list upcoming tournaments by game
- tournament(slug:$s) { events{ id name } } - get events in tournament  
- event(slug:tournament/X/event/Y) { id name } - get event by slug from URL
- event(id:$id) { sets(page,P,perPage,N,sortType:STANDARD){ pageInfo{total} nodes{id slots{id entrant{id name}}} } } - matches in event
- set(id:$id) { slots{id standing{id placement stats{score{label value}}}} } - set results
- set(id:$id) { state games{id orderNum winnerId entrant1Score entrant2Score stage{id name} selections{id character{id name}}} } - game details with chars/stages
- event(id:$id) { standings(query:{perPage:N,page:N}){ nodes{placement entrant{id name}} } } - standings
- videogames(query:{filter:{name:...},perPage:N}){ nodes{id name displayName} } - search games by name

GAME NAMING: Numeric IDs (Melee=1, Ultimate=287). name field for filtering, displayName human-readable. List at api.start.gg/videogames.
